g.text: Visual Breakdown: Grid with 4 Blocks × 8 Threads
threadIdx | uint3 | Thread index within block | 0 to blockDim-1
blockIdx | uint3 | Block index within grid | 0 to gridDim-1
blockDim | uint3 | Threads per block (from launch config)
gridDim | uint3 | Blocks in grid (from launch config)
Block | Thread in Block | Formula | Global Index

div.p: Why bounds check is essential: Grid size is rounded UP to cover all elements. If N=1000 and blockDim=256, we launch 1024 threads. Threads 1000-1023 would access invalid memory without this guard.

text.tspan: // ALWAYS check bounds for non-power-of-2 sizes!
// ALWAYS check bounds for non-power-of-2 sizes!
// threadIdx.x, threadIdx.y → position in block (0-15 each)
// blockIdx.x, blockIdx.y → block position in grid
// threadIdx.x, threadIdx.y → position in block (0-15 each)
// blockIdx.x, blockIdx.y → block position in grid

g.text: int idx = z * width * height + y * width + x;
if (x < W && y < H && z < D) {
